$meta.name: Mission Control — Atelier (pixel-RPG / guild)
$meta.completedExtraction: 2026-06-19 — added the two layers missed in the first pass (DR-056 complete-extraction): the app-wide RPG embossed skin (rpgSkin group) and the Party pixel-art spec (party group, incl. partyStructural). Core tokens unchanged.
$meta.provenance: Frozen FROM the owner-approved prototype prototype/index.html (+ party-proposal.html, party-pipeline.html) under DR-054 ADOPT-VISUAL. Mission Control's pixel-RPG identity is intentional — it is the factory's own internal tool, NOT a template for product apps.
$meta.supersedes: docs/design/brief.md (which wrongly invented a cold-blue hue-230 palette and demoted the prototype to a functional-only reference)
$meta.rule: The app's globals.css @theme mirrors this file. No hardcoded colors/spacing/radii in committed code. Tokens only.

trace.$note: Categorical, desaturated palette for the WoDag 2D DAG — one distinct color per highlighted dependency line so crossing lines stay traceable. Dark values; light overrides are darkened for AA contrast on the light canvas.
Trace palette darkened for AA contrast on the light canvas.

families_usage.pixel: Pixel/level numerals, XP and tier/rarity badges, the gamified RPG numerals — the pixel-RPG signature.
families_usage.display: Space Grotesk — display, headings (h1/h2/h3), buttons, tabs, nav, card titles.
families_usage.mono: Code, ids, command strings, paths (/pandacorp:* commands, REQ-NN ids, file paths) — tabular-nums.

spacing.notes: Compact, dense scale extracted from the prototype's padding/gap usage. Common card padding 10px; panel padding 16px 18px; gaps 6–12px.

pixelArt.appliesTo: Party zones (.mcstation .bg) and agent sprites (.mcag img), 54x54 sprites, 32px scene grid
pixelArt.note: Pixel-art is a first-class part of the contract (Party / La Fragua / La Campaña). Sprites and zone backgrounds MUST render with image-rendering: pixelated.

$meta.layer: App-wide RPG embossed skin — MISSED in the first extraction, added under DR-056 complete-extraction.
$meta.provenance: prototype/index.html lines 128-158, the GLOBAL skin block ('Skin RPG — GLOBAL (toda la app: una sola identidad)'). This is a SECOND-PASS override that re-skins the base components (.panel/.cfgcard/.navitem/.cmd) and adds the RPG primitives. The base .panel (panel component above) is the structural fallback; the skin below is what actually renders app-wide. Both must ship.
$meta.embossSignature: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255,255,255,.05), inset 0 -2px 0 rgba(0,0,0,.22), 0 2px 0 {surfaces.canvas} — a 1px top highlight + 2px inner-bottom shadow + 2px solid canvas-colored bottom ledge = the chunky 'pressed pixel tile' look.
$meta.rgbaAreLiteral: The rgba(255,255,255,.05) highlight and rgba(0,0,0,.22) inner-shadow are theme-independent structural literals (they work in both dark and light); keep them verbatim, do not tokenize to a surface color.

overrides.note: App-wide re-skin of base components. These REPLACE the base look defined in components.* above when the RPG skin is active (i.e. always, app-wide).

navitem.note: The .on (active) state KEEPS the base accentBg/accentText fill (navitem component above) and ADDS a 1px inset accent ring.

cmd.note: Command chips (/pandacorp:* strings) sit inset on the canvas color with a bd2 hairline.

rpgpanel.note: Identical surface to the skinned .panel; the explicit RPG panel primitive used by herostat/lockchip/shield containers.

rpggrid.note: The 22px embossed grid texture overlaid on RPG panels (distinct from the Party 32px/30px SCENE grids).

shield.note: The hero crest. 96x96 accent-bordered + glow medallion; typically carries the .rpggrid texture (class 'shield rpggrid').

stripes.note: 16px transparent + 2px canvas-colored notch = segmented XP-bar striping.

big.note: The big Pixelify-Sans stat numeral — the gamified RPG count (40px).

herostat.tierBadge: absolute top:11px right:12px; 9px; color {surfaces.canvas} on tier color; padding 1px 6px; radius 4px

reveal.note: The locked → hover/focus-reveal mechanic: a card overlay that fades in over the locked chip. Focus-within makes it keyboard-reachable.

secthead.note: Section header with a trailing 1px rule that fills remaining width.

itemslot.note: Inventory/equipment slot. Size is set inline per use (34/40/42/58px in the prototype); border 1.5–2px in the tier/accent color; background accentBg/warnBg/panel.

node.note: Tiny pixel node marker (skill-tree / mini sprite dots).

sprite.note: Generic pixel-art sprite base used inside RPG panels (the Party sub-views define their own larger sprite specs — see party group).

glowwarn.note: Warn-colored attention glow used on item slots/cards needing attention.

anim.rpgIn: from { opacity:0; transform: translateY(9px) } to { opacity:1; transform: translateY(0) }
anim.note: App-wide entrance animation for RPG panels: a 9px rise + fade, 340ms. Transform/opacity only; honors reduced-motion.

$meta.layer: Party pixel-art spec — MISSED in the first extraction, added under DR-056 complete-extraction. The two embedded sub-views are part of the contract.

subViews.index: prototype/index.html (the default Party canvas embedded in the dashboard — #rpg-scene, .mcstation, .mcag). Sprites 54px, scene grid 32px.
subViews.fragua: prototype/party-proposal.html — 'La Fragua' (build view). Sprites 52px, scene grid 30px, vault/forge rooms positioned on a 920x560 stage.
subViews.campana: prototype/party-pipeline.html — 'La Campaña' (pipeline view). Sprites 58px, scene grid 30px, phase rooms 250x208 on a 920x560 stage.

$meta.note: Per-view sprite sizes are recorded individually (index 54 / Fragua 52 / Campaña 58) — DO NOT average. A tokens-only build must be able to reproduce each view's backgrounds and sprite scale exactly.
$meta.rgbaScrimLiteral: The rgba(12,17,19,.NN) scrim family is theme-independent structural literal (dark map overlays). Keep verbatim.

partyStructural.$note: The ~10 hardcoded structural colors used to paint the Party map backgrounds (scene fill, grid lines, paths/connectors, vault floor, room scrims). These are NOT in the semantic palette; tokenize them as a dedicated group so a tokens-only build reproduces the backgrounds pixel-faithfully. Values are dark-map literals shared across all Party sub-views.

usage.sceneFillRadialA: Body radial-gradient stop A (Fragua body bg: radial 20% -10%).
usage.sceneFillRadialB: Body radial-gradient stop B (Fragua body bg: radial 100% 110%).
usage.sceneBase: Solid base color under the stage radial (.stage background base) and chip/doc backgrounds on the map.
usage.pathTrackLight: Light stripe of the dashed path/connector (.pathline / .conn .road repeating gradient, 9–10px).
usage.pathTrackDarkCampana: Dark stripe of the Campaña inactive .conn .road.
usage.pathSeam: inset 1px seam on path/connector tracks (box-shadow inset 0 0 0 1px).
usage.pathFlowLight: Light stripe of an ACTIVE/flowing connector (.conn.flow .road) in Campaña.
usage.pathFlowDarkCampana: Dark stripe of an active/flowing connector in Campaña.
usage.vaultFloorSeam: inset 1px seam on the vault floor (.vaultfloor, boveda.png).

scrim.$note: The rgba(12,17,19, alpha) scrim family — dark overlays for labels, counts, room darkening and vault gradients. Per-view alphas recorded.

spriteSizes_px.note: Per-view sprite render size (img width/height). index .mcag=54, Fragua .ag=52 (vault variant 42, sequential relay rstep 29), Campaña .sp=58 (small variant 46).

gridSizes_px.note: Scene grid cell. index #rpg-scene = 32px (var(--bd) .5px lines); Fragua/Campaña .stage::before = 30px (#1d2629 .5px lines). Distinct from the rpgSkin .rpggrid 22px panel texture.

roomSizes_px.note: Both redesigned sub-views use a 920x560 stage. Campaña phase rooms are fixed 250x208; Fragua forge/vault rooms are absolutely positioned (no single fixed room box). NOTE: the task brief cited a Fragua 432x372 room and a 32px Fragua grid — the CURRENT prototype (party-proposal.html, the approved redesign) uses a 920x560 stage with positioned rooms and a 30px grid; recorded as found. See 'ambiguity' in the design-decisions log.

behavior.split: img hidden, .relay sequence shown (sequential relay test→backend→contrato→frontend, 29px rstep sprites)

behavior.walking: .spi.walking animates walkbob .42s (the lead roams the room)

keyframes.$note: Complete keyframe inventory across the three Party sub-views. transform/opacity only; the Party canvas is where expressive motion is reserved (frequency test). Honor prefers-reduced-motion at the app shell.

index.mcsay: from {opacity 0, translateY(3px)} to {opacity 1, translateY(0)} (X-centered) — speech bubble in.

fragua.sayin: from {opacity 0, translateY(3px)} to {opacity 1, translateY(0)} (X-centered) — speech in.

campana.roompulse: 0%,100% {box-shadow 0 0 0 2px accent, 0 0 36px -10px accent} 50% {0 0 0 2px accent, 0 0 52px -6px accent} — active-room glow breathing.
campana.slidein: 0% {translateX(-3px) opacity .5} 50% {translateX(3px) opacity 1} 100% {translateX(-3px) opacity .5} — doc pip sliding along an active connector.
campana.sayin: from {opacity 0, translateY(3px)} to {opacity 1, translateY(0)} — speech in.

motion.rules: transform/opacity only, <300ms. Everyday motion sober; expressive (sprite bob, halos, emotes) reserved for the Party canvas. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.

assets.zones: prototype/assets/zones/*.png (12 pixel-art zones: research, frontend, backend, architecture, testing, review, security, analytics, release, boveda, build-hall, tribunal)
assets.staging: Must be copied into public/ for the Next.js app to serve them (see technical-assumptions.md).
